failure_modes.text: Teams can confuse surface familiarity with real capability when they try to evaluate or critique before enough analysis and understanding exist.
failure_modes.mitigation: Check which cognitive level the team has actually earned: can they explain the concept in plain language and apply it to a new case before judging quality?

failure_modes.text: A rigid staircase view can freeze learning when iteration, creativity, or revisiting earlier levels is actually required.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when the taxonomy is treated as a rigid staircase.
failure_modes.mitigation: Treat the levels as a diagnostic guide, not a gate: allow iteration between levels when the problem demands revisiting earlier understanding.

failure_modes.text: Overemphasis on lower stages can turn into analysis paralysis that never becomes application or creation.
failure_modes.mitigation: Impose a forcing function to move up: set a deadline for when understanding must become application, and hold the team to it.

premortem_questions.text: At which stage is this learner or team actually stuck: recall, understanding, application, analysis, evaluation, or synthesis?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Most useful when the work requires diagnosing whether someone is stuck at recall, understanding, application, or synthesis.

premortem_questions.text: Where does our explanation still fall apart, showing that we have not really built enough understanding to move upward yet?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Where does my explanation start to fall apart?

premortem_questions.text: Are we solving the right problem, or are we climbing stages on top of a flawed definition?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Are we solving the right problem-it may not be the one we were given.

heuristics.text: Sequence challenge so comprehension supports application, application supports analysis, and analysis supports later judgment and synthesis.
heuristics.source_quote: Works best when skill-building needs a progression from comprehension to execution to judgment.

heuristics.text: Use simple explanation and blind-spot checks before asking learners to make complex evaluative leaps.
Allow iteration back to earlier levels whenever real learning reveals that the foundation is still weak.
heuristics.source_quote: The presentation of the taxonomy as a literal, gated, and sequential process may overlook the reality that complex learning is often iterative and fluid.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on stage diagnosis, anti-premature-evaluation, and iterative progression rather than on broad curriculum theory.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor curriculum-design sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on diagnosing the next cognitive move.
open_questions: The source overlaps with creativity and strategy, but the intervention layer stays on stage fit and progression discipline.

allies: The raw allies section is strongest on systems-thinking, feynman-technique, and logical decomposition.
antagonists: The source is strongest against substitution-bias, cognitive overload, and premature critique.
structured_tensions: The model has clear tension around rigid sequencing versus iterative learning loops.
